summary: Full backend + frontend testing of Computer Champ kids educational game. Backend: 10/10 pytest tests passed across /api/, /api/students, /api/scores, /api/certificate/issue (incl. persistence, max-stars logic, badge awarding, 404s, and 400 validation). Frontend: all 11 critical UI flows passed (Welcome -> Levels -> 8 games -> Certificate -> auth redirect). Teacher Jyoti Singh photo renders, Class 1/2 buttons work, all data-testids present, 8 level cards visible on Level Map, Quiz shows exact 5 user questions (first question 'What is a computer?' confirmed, CPU option is 'Cabinet box' not brain), Jumble has 100+ words (107 unique), Speed timer starts at 45s, Spell/Jumble have letter/check/hint/reset(/skip) controls, Match has words+pics, Multi has yes/no, Picture has 3 options, Certificate shows student name, class, stars, and Jyoti Singh signature + download button. localStorage clear correctly redirects /levels -> /. Web Speech API calls do not crash in headless. html2canvas teacher image is flagged by browser CORS (expected per user note).

integration_issues.flow: Certificate teacher image rendering / html2canvas export
integration_issues.issue: Teacher mascot image on Certificate page is hosted on customer-assets.emergentagent.com and is fetched without CORS headers, so the request is blocked and the avatar inside the certificate preview shows a broken-image icon. html2canvas PNG export will also omit this image. User note flagged this as acceptable, but visual polish is affected. Consider serving the teacher image from the same origin (place file under /app/frontend/public/) or loading it via a backend proxy endpoint to inline it.

action_items: (Optional, P2) Host the teacher photo under /app/frontend/public/ (or proxy via backend) so it renders inside the Certificate preview and can be captured by html2canvas without CORS issues.

(Optional, P3) Learn Mode 'Next' button did not exit to /levels even after 40 consecutive clicks — verify there is a terminating 'Finish' state that routes back to /levels after the final topic; if by design (ended at last bullet with no nav), ignore.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: /api/certificate/issue does not check whether student_id exists before updating (update_one silently succeeds with 0 matches). Consider returning 404 when student not found.
server.py: POST /api/students accepts any student_class string — no validation that it is '1' or '2'. Low risk since UI restricts, but add Pydantic validator for defense in depth.
Backend file is small (~134 lines) and clean — no file-size or DRY violations. Async/await used correctly. MongoDB _id is excluded via projection on GET.
test_credentials: N/A — app has no login. Test student created via welcome flow using name 'TEST_Kid' and Class 1.
seed_data_creation: Created student 'TEST_TestKid' (class 1) in MongoDB during backend tests via POST /api/students. Not cleaned up (no DELETE endpoint exists). Volume is negligible.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend API is stable and fully tested in /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py — reuse as regression. Frontend uses data-testids consistent with review_request list. The only visual issue is the Certificate teacher image being CORS-blocked on the remote customer-assets CDN (user-acknowledged). When retesting, register student via welcome flow first (localStorage-backed) before visiting protected routes /learn /picture /quiz /match /spell /multitask /speed /jumble /certificate, else app redirects to /. Web Speech API has no voices in headless — speak() simply no-ops, which is expected.
rca of the issue: Certificate teacher avatar CORS: the element is tagged with crossOrigin='anonymous' (needed for html2canvas to include it in the PNG). customer-assets.emergentagent.com does not return Access-Control-Allow-Origin, so the browser discards the decoded pixels and the tag renders as broken. Reproduction: navigate to /certificate after registering -> small broken-image icon appears inside the gold circle near 'Jyoti Singh'. Mitigation: move teacher photo to /app/frontend/public/jyoti.jpg and reference as '/jyoti.jpg', or proxy through FastAPI endpoint that streams the image with CORS headers.
